deferredRowDetails.boundaryRule: Keep visible as a refusal boundary only; if surfaced in relation UI, route back into thanks or the direct repair lane.

nextPassExpansionCluster.summary: One ordered direct-pickup cluster covering map-showing, hotel arrival and room issue, then card payment follow-up.

rowDetails.packetOutcomeReason: Strongest map-showing bridge back into the starter directions pair; advance it into the next prep-only slice while still carrying directions confidence review.
Hotel-arrival anchor for the next prep-only slice; keep it adjacent to check-in while hotel-language review continues.
Arrival-pair companion to reservation; advance it in the same next prep-only slice instead of separating the pair.
Keep the first room-issue branch visible, but `aircon` wording still needs the stricter hotel-language review surface before it advances.
Keep the strongest payment follow-on visible and retrievable, but outside the immediate next slice while payment-language review remains open.

directPickupContract.summary: One ordered five-row direct-pickup contract that must stay adjacent and review-bound until runtime-safe promotion blockers are resolved.
directPickupContract.packetOutcomeRule: `promote-now` means advance into the next prep-only runtime/content working slice. It does not mean runtime-safe promotion, live readiness, or app wiring readiness.
directPickupContract.promotionRule: Keep all five rows outside runtime-safe promotion until their blocker surfaces are resolved or explicitly rejected in order.

packetOutcomeRows.why: Strongest map-showing bridge back into the starter directions pair.
Hotel-arrival anchor for the next prep-only slice.
Advance with reservation so the arrival pair stays intact.
Keep visible, but `aircon` wording still needs the stricter hotel-language review surface before it advances.
Useful payment follow-on that stays visible outside the immediate next slice while payment-language review remains open.

blockerSurfaces.why: Map-showing utility is strong, but native confidence review still needs to confirm the directions wording before promotion.
Reservation, check-in, and aircon wording should move as one hotel lane and stay outside promotion until the hotel-language review lands.
Card-payment wording remains useful but still needs payment-language review before promotion.

directPickupContract.laterOnlySeparationRule: Do not reopen the later-only hold rows until the full five-row direct-pickup contract is promoted or explicitly rejected in order.

immediateTrust: Preserve the five-row direct-pickup contract exactly as the ordered retrieval boundary.
When the lane expands beyond the 16 starter rows, advance only `tagalog-directions-1`, `tagalog-hotel-hostel-1`, and `tagalog-hotel-hostel-2` first.
stillNeedsValidation: Pronunciation posture review for the expanded Tagalog lane.
noLongerNeedsRediscovery: Which one row stays deferred for native/expert review.
That `tagalog-polite-basics-4` remains the deferred refusal boundary.
That `tagalog-simple-problems-7` and `tagalog-grab-taxi-7` remain the only later-only holds.
That Tagalog is still `prepared-next`, not live.

laterOnlyHold.unlockRule: Reopen these only after the five-row direct-pickup contract is promoted or explicitly rejected in order.

graduationBoundary.summary: The Tagalog lane is not runtime-safe to promote yet. Reopening later-only hold rows is separate from safe promotion.

promotionBlockedBy.why: The refusal row stays visible but deferred until native review confirms it works as a polite decline in sales or offer contexts.
The map-showing directions row remains the first direct-pickup candidate, but it still needs native confidence before promotion.
The hotel arrival and room-issue rows stay as one review lane until their wording is normalized together.
Card-payment wording remains outside promotion until payment-language review lands.
The call-for-me escalation row remains later-only because register and pronoun risk are still higher than the direct-pickup set.
Pronunciation remains helper-only draft guidance and is not yet a locked traveler-facing contract.

graduationBoundary.laterOnlyHoldRule: Keep `tagalog-simple-problems-7` and `tagalog-grab-taxi-7` visible but out of the next pass until the five-row direct-pickup contract is promoted or explicitly rejected in order.
graduationBoundary.stopCondition: Keep the lane prep-only until the deferred refusal row and all five direct-pickup rows either clear review or are explicitly rejected, and do not treat later-only reopen eligibility as runtime-safe promotion.

handoffContract.rowOutcomeCsvOrder: Rows 1-16 are starter-core anchors, row 17 is the deferred refusal boundary, rows 18-22 are ordered direct-pickup contract rows, and rows 23-24 remain later-only holds.

scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Cafe menus often use English terms but a short Tagalog phrase still helps when the shop is busy.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: Pointing helps at busy stalls but simple Tagalog is useful for spice level water and takeout.

scenarios.name: Grab / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Grab is common in major cities and showing the destination on your phone helps drivers quickly.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Ask the price before agreeing and keep small bills ready when shopping at markets or street stalls.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: A warm tone and a simple greeting go a long way in hotels shops rides and casual conversations.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: Mini-marts are good for water tissues sunscreen and quick snacks during a long day out.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Keep your passport and booking details ready because front desks usually register guests at check-in.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Save your hotel name and one landmark in your phone so you can ask for help even if maps fail.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: If something goes wrong stay calm and show booking screens photos or screenshots to speed up help.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Short friendly chat often starts with where you are from whether it is your first visit and what food you like.

notes: Canonical drafting baseline for Tagalog parity with the Viet 10-scenario traveler surface, plus a retrieval-ready handoff contract for the current starter, deferred, direct-pickup, later-only, and graduation-boundary rules.
